pattern: For each value in nums2 scaled by k, iterate over its multiples up to max(nums1) and count how many times each multiple appears in nums1 using a frequency table.
hints: A pair (i,j) is good when nums1[i] is divisible by nums2[j]*k; naively O(n*m) is too slow here.
Build a frequency map of nums1; for each distinct value v in nums2, count multiples of v*k that appear in nums1 by stepping through v*k, 2*v*k, 3*v*k, ....
The total work is proportional to sum over all nums2 values of (max_nums1 / (v*k)), which is fast for large k or large v.

edge_inputs.description: no good pairs because k makes product exceed all nums1
single elements that form a good pair
nums2 has duplicates; each copy counts separately
nums1 has many copies of same divisible value

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(size, 500)
    nums1 = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    nums2 = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 10)
    return [nums1, nums2, k]
